shootDirection: a graffiti-covered back alley, the group mid-conversation walking toward a doorway camera - candid, not posed
concept: Graffiti alley doorway walk-up, tight candid group portrait
scene: In a narrow graffiti-covered back alley at blue hour, the four selected artists are caught mid-conversation walking toward a metal side doorway just a few steps from the camera, framed tightly enough that every face is large, crisp, and detailed while their bodies overlap naturally in motion. On the left, the young woman in the white shirt and black tie turns slightly inward as if listening, her dark hair bouncing softly; beside her, the auburn-haired woman in sculptural glasses and a dark outfit leans forward half a step, speaking with an intent but warm expression. Slightly deeper in the frame, the dark-haired man in the black high-neck top glances sideways toward them rather than at the lens, one shoulder brushing the tagged brick wall, while on the right the masked figure with bleached hair emerges through a haze of cool doorway light, the glowing X-eyed mask adding a surreal note against otherwise real street styling. The alley is damp and softly reflective, the pastel grade muting the spray-paint reds and blues into chalky tones, with diffused dusk light from above and a faint spill from the doorway creating calm contrast instead of harsh drama; the group feels intimate, unscripted, and genuinely in the same moment rather than posed in a lineup.
prompt: Create one original, photorealistic 16:9 group photograph that brings together the musicians shown across the 4 reference images into a single shared real-world scene. Scene: In a narrow graffiti-covered back alley at blue hour, the four selected artists are caught mid-conversation walking toward a metal side doorway just a few steps from the camera, framed tightly enough that every face is large, crisp, and detailed while their bodies overlap naturally in motion. On the left, the young woman in the white shirt and black tie turns slightly inward as if listening, her dark hair bouncing softly; beside her, the auburn-haired woman in sculptural glasses and a dark outfit leans forward half a step, speaking with an intent but warm expression. Slightly deeper in the frame, the dark-haired man in the black high-neck top glances sideways toward them rather than at the lens, one shoulder brushing the tagged brick wall, while on the right the masked figure with bleached hair emerges through a haze of cool doorway light, the glowing X-eyed mask adding a surreal note against otherwise real street styling. The alley is damp and softly reflective, the pastel grade muting the spray-paint reds and blues into chalky tones, with diffused dusk light from above and a faint spill from the doorway creating calm contrast instead of harsh drama; the group feels intimate, unscripted, and genuinely in the same moment rather than posed in a lineup. Keep every person clearly recognisable and faithful to their reference photo - preserve each face, skin tone, hair, facial hair, glasses, and overall likeness exactly as shown. Do not invent anyone who is not in the reference photos. Keep this an intimate, well-composed group, not a crowd. Where a reference photo shows a band, feature only its single most recognisable member rather than every member. Frame the group tightly - close or medium-close - so every face is large in the frame, sharp, and richly detailed, never small or distant. Arrange them naturally within the scene as one connected group sharing the same moment and light, like a real posed or candid group photo. Vary posture, depth, and where people are looking - this is a caught moment, not a band-publicity lineup evenly spaced and facing the camera. Not separate cut-outs floating side by side, and never a grid, row, or panel of squares. Render photorealistically: real skin texture, true-to-life lighting and depth of field, captured as if shot on a professional camera. This is a real photograph, not an illustration, drawing, painting, cartoon, or render. Colour treatment: soft muted pastel grade, gentle and calm rather than saturated, drawing on powder blue, dusty pink, soft charcoal, chalk white, muted lavender. Mood: intimate, cool, quietly magnetic. Do not add any extra people beyond the reference subjects. Do not duplicate, merge, blend, or distort faces. Do not include any text, letters, words, numbers, captions, titles, logos, watermarks, or signage anywhere in the image. Avoid: readable text, letters, words, numbers, captions, titles, logos, watermarks, signage, grid layout, contact sheet, evenly tiled squares, raw album-cover thumbnails, collage of separate panels, illustration, illustrated, drawing, painting, cartoon, comic, anime, vector art, sketch, flat graphic style, extra people, background crowd, duplicated faces, cloned faces, merged faces, blended faces, morphed features, distorted faces, deformed hands, extra limbs. Photorealistic, sharp, high quality, flattering, with real depth and a strong sense of place.
